author: @garrenosborne9623
text:
The basic basics ....ordering is significant, even when issues have a cyclical component.
Energy'
 1. Use 
(it doesn't exist in a vacuum ... Socioeconomic factors always present ...market farces ...I mean forces) 

2. Scale time/population
3. Generation - fuel,  development lag, comparative total impacts 
4.transmission / location
5.storage
6. Integration across whole economy (etc wast industrial heat going into district heating with least conversion re conversion  loss (electricity / heat / electric / heat with losses each stage)
7. Decommissioning design for disassembly DfD / BaMB (building as materials bank)

author: @kenb7051
text: Yeah but how much energy is used to heat homes and buildings during winter? It would guess it  to far exceed AC.

replies.text: AC is way less efficient than heating, when you're talking raw electrical power. humans passively generate heat, as do circuits and the most populous areas of the world are more hot than they are cold, so it's not as much

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text:
In some sense , you're right - we should look at things from different angles (which is bloody difficult , if someone is stone rooted in his/hers beliefs ). Of course , broader picture helps. But , the devil is in the details. Do we (human kind)  have an energy problem ? No , we don't.  We have a problem with distribution of produced energy and , yes , storing of excess energy. Than we come to question of who controls it and all nice theory s and ideas are lost. Decentralization of energy production and distribution is just a small step in a right direction , but still not a major part of solution. Capabilities and possibilities vary across the world and not necessarily in accordance to the needs of particular region/country (in terms of population , concentration of that population , industrial and developing potential...). Also , we will never convince people to give up their comforts willingly (air conditioning..) , unless something big happens (like war , earthquake , massive meteor impact...) and just cuts supply and production , so people are forced to live according "needs - must" philosophy . 
 Ideas and principals and (sometimes) unorthodox solutions to the various problems , that you're presenting on this channel are very valuable. There is , also , an entertainment value to it. I don't get why do you explain why are you doing something , on your channel , so many times . I mean , if someone is late to the party and asks where all the snacks and beer is gone - let him think for itself why and how.

author: @industrialmonk
text: I have spent most of my life from childhood repairing modifying or salvaging part's to make & just making things sometimes I make part's due to none available. I get a thrill out of it & it's so nice to have things I have made/repaired.

author: @MountainCatBob
text:
Yes! The first obstacle to overcome is the idea that the best solution already exists. And your philosophy of thinking Small is Brilliant! 
I bought a Neptune 4 and printed some of the models you posted -- the really cool Spheroid and Betz dual blade turbines. Great projects for wintry weather! Thanks for posting those terrific design files!
